body.p: S1000D is an international specification for the procurement and production of technical publications (mainly used in aerospace and aviation industries). It is an XML-based specification for preparing, managing, and using equipment maintenance and operations information.

S1000D is articulated based on three main notions:
Data Module - It is an XML file that defines a standalone information unit.
Data Module Structure - It defines how a Data Module is divided:
The Identification and Status part that identifies the Data Module within the CSDB structure.
The Content part that is the detailed information of the Data Module.
Common Source DataBase (CSDB) - It defines how the Data Modules are arranged inside a publication.
includes built-in support for S1000D. You can work with S1000D issues 4.0, 4.1, and 4.2 using built-in support for editing, visual authoring, templates, BREX handling, and smart paste.

section.title: Default Document Templates
section.p: Create new S1000D data modules or publication modules for issues 4.0, 4.1, and 4.2 using the provided file templates available when creating new documents from templatesnew documents from templates.

section.title: Default Schema for Validation and Content Completion
section.p: Edit S1000D documents with schema-guided editing, validation, and content completion support. View documentation for S1000D elements and attributes directly in the content completion window and in the Attributes view.

section.title: Author Mode Actions
section.p: Visually edit S1000D documents in the Author page and switch between full-width, print preview, or large-font styles. The following dedicated actions are available in the toolbar for editing S1000D documents:

dlentry.dd: Applies bold styling to the selected text.
Applies italic styling to the selected text.
Applies underline styling to the selected text.
Applies overline styling to the selected text.
Applies strikethrough styling to the selected text.
Applies subscript styling to the selected text.
Applies superscript styling to the selected text.
Inserts a list item at the cursor position.
Inserts a sequential list at the cursor position.
Inserts a simple random (unordered) list at the cursor position.
Inserts an unordered random list at the cursor position.
Inserts a definition list at the cursor position.
Opens a dialog box that allows you to configure and insert a table.
Inserts a table row below the current row.
Inserts a table column after the current column.

section.title: BREX Editing and Transformation
section.p: Edit BREX modules in the Text or Author page with content completion and validation support. You can also convert BREX modules to Schematron using the provided transformation scenario.

section.title: Smart Paste
section.p: Paste content from web browsers or Word documents into S1000D documents while preserving structure and formatting.

body.note: To display the CGM images, you have to install the CGM Add-on.
body.p: If you want more advanced S1000D editing features, you can ask some of our partners.
